claims.claim: Users with permission to *Read* a page can view its version history
claims.evidence: WebPageVersionsBase.cs line 100: ConfigurePage() calls CheckAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.READ). Line 149-151: CanUserDisplayVersions() returns HasAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.READ). Both confirm READ is the required permission to view versions.

claims.claim: Users with permission to *Update* a page can restore its previous versions
claims.evidence: WebPageVersionsBase.cs line 142-144: CanUserRestoreContentVersion() returns HasAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.UPDATE). Line 199: RestoreVersion() calls CheckAclPermission(WebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.UPDATE). READ is also implicitly required (line 100 of ConfigurePage), but as documented on the hub page, Read is a prerequisite for all page permissions except Display.

claims.location: Section: Permissions, Pages row (line 57), new cross-reference

claims.claim: Cross-reference anchor 'Edit page permissions' exists in the target page
claims.location: Section: Permissions, Pages row (line 57), new cross-reference
claims.evidence: page-permission-management.md line 132: '## Edit page permissions' — heading exists as a valid anchor target

claims.claim: Cross-reference link text 'page permission management' is descriptive (per REC-005)
claims.location: Section: Permissions, Pages row (line 57), new cross-reference
claims.evidence: REC-005 requires descriptive link text. 'page permission management' tells the reader what they'll find at the target. REC-005 is compatible with INV-crossref-009 (no conflict).

claims.claim: Read and Update permissions for pages can be configured for specific roles
claims.location: Section: Permissions, Pages row (line 57), new cross-reference sentence context
claims.evidence: page-permission-management.md 'Edit page permissions' section (lines 132-148) describes breaking inheritance and setting permissions per role. The permission table (lines 26-104) includes Read and Update as configurable page permissions.

claims.location: Section: Permissions, introductory paragraph (line 41) and table cells
claims.evidence: content-versioning.md (business-users) line 3: identifier: page_versioning_xp. File path: src/_documentation/_documentation/business-users/content-versioning.md

claims.claim: Anchor 'View version history' exists in page_versioning_xp target
Anchor 'Restore previously published versions' exists in page_versioning_xp target
claims.evidence: content-versioning.md (business-users) line 82: '## Restore previously published versions'

claims.claim: Users with the *View* permission for a workspace in Content hub can view version history for all items in that workspace
claims.location: Section: Permissions, Reusable content items row (line 65)
claims.evidence: ContentItemVersions.cs class attribute: [WorkspaceEvaluatePermission(WorkspaceDataPermissions.VIEW)]. CanUserDisplayVersions() at line ~180 checks WorkspaceDataPermissions.VIEW via workspacePermissionEvaluator.

claims.claim: Users with *Update* permission for a workspace can restore previous versions of items in that workspace
claims.location: Section: Permissions, Reusable content items row (line 65)
claims.evidence: ContentItemVersions.cs CanUserRestoreContentVersion() checks WorkspaceDataPermissions.UPDATE via workspacePermissionEvaluator. RestoreVersion() calls CheckRestorePermission() which throws ForbiddenAccessException if not authorized.

claims.claim: Users with permission to *View* a headless channel application can view version history for all items in that channel
claims.evidence: HeadlessItemVersions.cs class attribute: [UIEvaluatePermission(SystemPermissions.VIEW)]. CanUserDisplayVersions() checks SystemPermissions.VIEW via uiPermissionEvaluator.

claims.claim: Users with *Update* permission for a headless channel application can restore previous versions of items in this channel
claims.evidence: HeadlessItemVersions.cs CanUserRestoreContentVersion() checks SystemPermissions.UPDATE. RestoreVersion() has [PageCommand(Permission = SystemPermissions.UPDATE)] attribute.

claims.claim: An item can be restored when the item is in any workflow step
claims.evidence: All three RestoreVersion() implementations (WebPageVersionsBase line 197, ContentItemVersions, HeadlessItemVersions) do not check workflow step or CanWorkWithContentItemInCurrentStep. They only check ACL/workspace UPDATE permission, then proceed with TryCreateDraft/TryUpdateDraft/StartNewWorkflowCycle regardless of current step.

claims.claim: Users can restore a previous version of an item even when the item is in a step they don't have permission for
claims.evidence: RestoreVersion() implementations do not call CanWorkWithContentItemInCurrentStep (unlike save, changeWorkflowStep, discard operations which do). The only permission check is ACL UPDATE (web pages) or workspace/application UPDATE (content items, headless).

claims.claim: The item is restored to the *Draft* step with the data from the selected version
claims.evidence: WebPageVersionsBase.RestoreVersion() lines 218-227: TryCreateDraft() creates/moves to Draft, TryUpdateDraft() applies the version data, StartNewWorkflowCycle() resets the workflow. Same pattern in ContentItemVersions and HeadlessItemVersions RestoreVersion() methods.

claims.claim: The item has to go through the regular workflow from the start
claims.evidence: All RestoreVersion() implementations call StartNewWorkflowCycle() after TryCreateDraft/TryUpdateDraft, which resets the workflow cycle. The item must then advance through workflow steps normally from Draft.

researchRecommendationChecks.notes: REC-005 recommends descriptive link text. The new cross-reference uses 'page permission management' as link text, which tells readers what they'll find. No conflict with invariants (INV-crossref-009 compatible).

domainInsightChecks.notes: Multi-layer permission architecture: the content correctly differentiates the page ACL layer (Read/Update) from the application-level permission (Access channel, implicit). No disambiguation issue present.
